seo.title: Chiang Mai Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Chiang Mai travel guide for 2026. 5-day itinerary, transport from Bangkok, must-try food, budget breakdown, and cultural tips from experienced travelers.

intro: Chiang Mai rewards travelers who take their time. Five days is the sweet spot — enough to explore the Old City temples, trek to a waterfall, eat your weight in khao soi, and still have a day for an elephant sanctuary or Doi Inthanon. This guide covers everything from getting there to daily budgets, with practical tips that save you time and money.

itinerary.title: 5 Days in Chiang Mai: The Complete Itinerary

days.title: Old City Temples & Sunday Market
activities: Morning: Walk to Wat Chedi Luang (40 THB) — the massive 14th-century ruined chedi is Chiang Mai's most impressive archaeological site
Mid-morning: Continue to Wat Phra Singh (free) for classic Lanna architecture and the revered Phra Singh Buddha image
Lunch: Khao soi at Khao Soi Khun Yai near the north gate (50 THB) — arrive before noon as they sell out
Afternoon: Explore Wat Chiang Man (oldest temple, founded 1296) and the quiet lanes of the Old City's northeast corner
Evening: Sunday Walking Street Market on Ratchadamnoen Road (4 PM–midnight) — eat sai ua sausage, browse Lanna crafts

days.title: Doi Suthep & Nimman Exploration
activities: Morning: Take a songthaew to Wat Phra That Doi Suthep (60 THB shared). Climb the 306 naga steps and enjoy panoramic city views
Detour: Stop at Wat Pha Lat (hidden jungle temple) on the way down via the Monk's Trail — free and serene
Lunch: Cafe-hop in Nimmanhaemin — try Ristr8to (World Latte Art Champion) on Soi 3
Afternoon: Browse the boutiques and art galleries along Nimman Sois 1–9
Evening: Dinner and drinks at one of Nimman's rooftop bars

days.title: Ethical Elephant Sanctuary
activities: Full day: Visit Elephant Nature Park or Elephant Jungle Sanctuary (1,800–3,500 THB including transport and lunch)
Morning: Feed elephants their morning breakfast of bananas and watermelons
Midday: Walk alongside elephants through the forest to the river
Afternoon: Mud bath and river bathing with the elephants — the highlight of the day
Evening: Relaxed dinner at a riverside restaurant — try The Riverside Bar & Restaurant for live music

days.title: Doi Inthanon Day Trip
activities: Early morning: Depart 7 AM for Doi Inthanon National Park (300 THB entrance, 90 km drive)
Morning: Hike the Kew Mae Pan nature trail through cloud forest (3 km loop, guide required)
Midday: Visit the twin royal chedis and their stunning gardens at the summit
Afternoon: Stop at Wachirathan waterfall on the way down — powerful cascade you can walk behind
Evening: Night Bazaar along Chang Khlan Road — nightly market with souvenirs and street food

days.title: Cooking Class & Local Markets
activities: Morning: Thai cooking class starting with a guided market tour (900–1,500 THB for half day)
Learn to cook 4–5 dishes: pad thai, green curry, tom yum, mango sticky rice, and papaya salad
Afternoon: Explore Warorot Market (Kad Luang) — Chiang Mai's biggest local market near the Ping River
Late afternoon: Traditional Thai massage at Lila Thai Massage (200–300 THB/hour) — well-earned after 5 days
Evening: Farewell dinner with a view — SP Chicken near the north gate for the city's best grilled chicken

transport.fromBangkok: Flights from Bangkok to Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX) take 1 hour and cost from 1,200 THB one-way on AirAsia, Nok Air, or Thai Lion Air. Overnight sleeper trains from Bangkok's Hua Lamphong station take 12–14 hours with upper berths from 800 THB and lower berths from 1,000 THB — the scenery north of Phitsanulok is spectacular at dawn. VIP buses from Mo Chit terminal take 9–10 hours (from 550 THB) with onboard toilets and reclining seats.
localTransport: Red songthaews: Shared red trucks cruising main roads — 30 THB/person within the Old City, negotiate for longer distances
Grab: Southeast Asia's Uber — motorbike rides 30–60 THB, cars 80–200 THB. Most reliable option
Scooter rental: 150–400 THB/day. International driving permit technically required. Always wear a helmet
Tuk-tuks: 60–150 THB per trip — always agree on the price before getting in
Bicycles: Many guesthouses offer free bikes, or rent for 50–100 THB/day. Old City is flat and bikeable
Chartered songthaew: 800–1,500 THB for day trips to Doi Suthep, Doi Inthanon, or Bua Tong waterfalls

mustTry: Khao Soi — coconut curry noodle soup with crispy noodle topping (the definitive Chiang Mai dish)
Sai Ua — northern Thai sausage loaded with lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime
Khao Kha Moo — braised pork leg on rice with Chinese five-spice and boiled egg
Khanom Jeen Nam Ngiao — rice noodles in spicy tomato-pork broth with blood cubes
Laab Moo Kua — dry-fried minced pork with toasted rice powder and fresh herbs
Nam Prik Ong — northern Thai chili dip with minced pork, served with sticky rice and raw vegetables
Mango Sticky Rice — the classic Thai dessert, especially good during mango season (April–June)
foodAreas: Chang Phueak Gate night stalls — the famous cowboy hat lady (Khao Kha Moo) plus dozens of food carts
Chiang Mai Gate Market — morning khao kha moo and jok (rice porridge), evening grilled sausage and satay
Warorot Market — local market with dried fruits, sausages, and northern Thai snacks
Nimman Soi 9 — mix of Thai street food and hip international restaurants
Nong Hoi Market — off-the-tourist-trail morning market with the best prices
tips: Follow the locals — if a stall has a queue of Thai people, the food is good and safe
Khao soi restaurants typically close by 2–3 PM — eat it for lunch, not dinner
Ask for 'mai pet' (not spicy) if you can't handle heat — northern Thai food is generally milder than southern
Street food is safe in Chiang Mai — look for vendors who cook to order with visible fresh ingredients
Try the northern Thai set meal (khantoke dinner) at Old Chiang Mai Cultural Center for a cultural food experience

etiquette: Always remove shoes before entering temples — look for the pile of shoes at the entrance
Cover shoulders and knees at temples. Sarongs and wraps are available to borrow at major temples
Never touch someone's head — it's considered the most sacred part of the body in Thai culture
Don't point your feet at Buddha images or people — tuck them underneath when sitting in temples
Wai (palms together bow) when greeting elders, monks, or service staff — it's deeply appreciated in the north
Stand still during the national anthem played at 8 AM and 6 PM in public areas
Ask permission before photographing monks or hill tribe people — most are happy to oblige
Speak softly and avoid public displays of anger — maintaining calm (jai yen) is core to Thai culture

budget.budget: 600–1,000 THB/day (~$17–28 USD): Dorm bed 200–350 THB, street food meals 40–80 THB each, free temple visits, songthaew transport 30 THB/ride, 1-hour Thai massage 200 THB. Chiang Mai is one of Southeast Asia's best budget destinations.
budget.midRange: 2,000–4,000 THB/day (~$56–112 USD): Boutique hotel 1,500–3,000 THB, mix of street food and restaurant meals 200–500 THB, cooking class or elephant sanctuary 1,500–3,000 THB, Grab rides and occasional tuk-tuk 200–400 THB.
budget.luxury: 6,000–15,000+ THB/day (~$168–420+ USD): 5-star hotel or resort 4,000–18,000 THB, fine dining and rooftop bars 1,000–3,000 THB, private guided tours 3,000–8,000 THB, spa treatments 2,000–5,000 THB, chartered transport.
